---
description: Automatically transforms BDD specification documents into a C4 Model architecture design and a formal TLA+ specification with TLC model checking. Runs after /specification completes.
name: spec-to-architecture
---

# Spec-to-Architecture: BDD to C4 + TLA+ Pipeline

Automatically invoked after `/specification` completes. Reads BDD `.feature` files from the docs directory, generates a C4 Model architecture design (SWEBOK Ch 2/3), and produces a raw TLA+ formal specification (SWEBOK Ch 11) with TLC model checking. This skill is non-interactive — it operates entirely on the artifacts produced by the prior `/specification` phase.

## Quick Decision Tree

```
Which task?
├─ Generate architecture design (C4 diagrams) → architecture-reference.md
├─ Generate TLA+ formal specification → tla-plus-reference.md
├─ Run TLC model checker → tla-plus-reference.md
└─ Map BDD scenarios to TLA+ elements → tla-plus-reference.md
```

## When to Invoke

This skill is triggered automatically when `/specification` finishes successfully and writes BDD documents to `/docs/<feature-name>/bdd/`. It is NOT invoked via user command. If the specification directory does not exist, the skill halts and informs the user to run `/specification` first.

## Pipeline Stages

The skill executes these stages in order. Each stage depends on the output of the previous stage.

1. **Stage 1 — Validate Inputs**: Check that `/docs/<feature-name>/bdd/` exists and contains at least one valid `.feature` file. Halt with instructions if missing.
1. **Stage 2 — Parse BDD**: Parse all `.feature` files (normal-course, exceptions, boundaries) into structured scenarios. Auto-correct malformed Gherkin where possible.
1. **Stage 3 — Generate Architecture**: Extract actors, systems, containers, and quality attributes from parsed BDD. Generate C4 System Context and Container diagrams (Mermaid) and write architecture/ files.
1. **Stage 4 — Extract TLA+ Model**: Map BDD scenarios to TLA+ constructs. Build VARIABLES from Given clauses, action predicates from Then clauses, temporal properties from sequenced scenarios.
1. **Stage 5 — Generate TLA+ Module**: Write `<feature>.tla` with CONSTANTS, VARIABLES, Init, Next, Spec, INVARIANTS, and temporal properties. Generate `<feature>.cfg` with model values.
1. **Stage 6 — Run TLC**: Execute `java -cp tla2tools.jar tlc2.TLC -workers 4 -model <feature>`. Monitor for unbounded state space growth.
1. **Stage 7 — Auto-Fix Loop**: On TLC failure, diagnose, modify the TLA+ spec, and re-run. Continue while progress is made. Stop and report to the user if no convergence.
1. **Stage 8 — Generate Reports**: Write tla-plus/README.md with results and unmapped scenario log. Write architecture/README.md with traceability.

## Output Directory Structure

```
docs/<feature-name>/
  ├── requirements.md              (from /specification)
  ├── bdd/                         (from /specification)
  │   ├── normal-course.feature
  │   ├── exceptions.feature
  │   └── boundaries.feature
  ├── README.md                    (updated by this skill)
  ├── architecture/                (generated by Stage 3)
  │   ├── system-context.md        # C4 Level 1 + Mermaid diagram
  │   ├── container.md             # C4 Level 2 + Mermaid diagram
  │   └── README.md                # Architecture overview + quality attribute mapping
  └── tla-plus/                    (generated by Stages 4–7)
      ├── <feature>.tla             # Raw TLA+ module
      ├── <feature>.cfg             # TLC configuration
      ├── TLC-output.log            # Raw TLC output
      └── README.md                 # Explanation + unmapped scenarios
```

## Failure Mode Handling

| Failure | Behavior |
| --- | --- |
| Specification directory missing | Halt. Inform user to run /specification first. |
| Empty BDD feature files | Halt with warning. No verifiable behavior found. |
| Malformed Gherkin | Attempt auto-correction. Continue if fixed. Report if not. |
| Unmappable BDD scenarios | Log as unmapped. Continue with mappable scenarios. |
| TLA+ syntax error | Diagnose, fix, re-run TLC. |
| Invariant violation | Analyze counterexample, modify spec, re-run TLC. |
| Deadlock detected | Determine if expected. If not, add fairness or modify Next. |
| TLC unbounded state | Terminate TLC. Report partial results. Recommend smaller CONSTANTS. |
| Auto-fix no convergence | Stop loop. Report current state and TLC output to user. |
| tla2tools.jar missing | Report missing dependency. Still generate .tla and .cfg files. |

## Skill Resources

This skill provides the following reference documents. When the agent needs detailed domain theory, architectural guidance, or TLA+ formal specification techniques, it MUST read the corresponding file from this skill's resources/ directory.

| Topic | Reference Document | Description / Keywords |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C4 Model Architecture Design | [architecture-reference.md](resources/architecture-reference.md) | C4 Context + Container levels, Mermaid diagrams, quality attribute mapping, SWEBOK Ch 2/3 compliance |
| TLA+ Formal Specification | [tla-plus-reference.md](resources/tla-plus-reference.md) | TLA+ module structure, BDD-to-TLA+ extraction, TLC config generation, auto-fix loop, SWEBOK Ch 11 |

## SWEBOK v4 References

This skill directly references the following SWEBOK v4 chapters from the `swebok` skill. When the agent needs detailed domain theory, conceptual foundations, or compliance checklists, it MUST read the corresponding file from the swebok skill directory.

* [requirements-fundamentals.md](../swebok/resources/requirements-fundamentals.md) — Requirements traceability from BDD to architecture and TLA+.
* [architecture-fundamentals.md](../swebok/resources/architecture-fundamentals.md) — Architecture design stage, prescriptive vs descriptive architecture, stakeholders.
* [architecture-description.md](../swebok/resources/architecture-description.md) — C4 Model as an architecture view, ADLs, architecture patterns.
* [design-fundamentals.md](../swebok/resources/design-fundamentals.md) — Design principles (abstraction, modularization, separation of concerns) applied to architecture.
* [testing-fundamentals.md](../swebok/resources/testing-fundamentals.md) — Traceability from requirements to test cases (TLC verification as testing).
* [models-methods-modeling.md](../swebok/resources/models-methods-modeling.md) — Modeling formalisms, state machines, preconditions/postconditions/invariants.
* [models-methods-types.md](../swebok/resources/models-methods-types.md) — Structural and behavioral modeling types for TLA+ extraction.

## Compliance Checklist

* Was the specification directory validated before processing?
* Were all three BDD feature files (normal-course, exceptions, boundaries) parsed?
* Does the architecture contain both System Context and Container C4 diagrams?
* Are quality attributes from non-functional requirements mapped to architectural decisions?
* Does the TLA+ module include CONSTANTS, VARIABLES, Init, Next, Spec, and INVARIANTS?
* Does the TLC config assign concrete model values to all CONSTANTS?
* Were unmappable scenarios logged with reasons?
* Was TLC executed and results captured?
* Was the auto-fix loop monitored for convergence?
* Does the README contain traceability from BDD scenarios to architecture and TLA+ elements?

Do not produce any conversational output or solicit user input during pipeline execution. This skill is fully automated and operates on /specification artifacts.
